description: Phase 1 Sample Test Cases for Lane Following Reward Function
note: These scenarios represent different driving situations. Use these to understand how the reward function scores driving behavior.

test_cases.name: Perfect Center
test_cases.description: Car is dead center on track with no steering

test_cases.name: Slightly Off-Center
test_cases.description: Car is close to center (within 10% threshold) with gentle steering

test_cases.name: Approaching Center
test_cases.description: Car is within 25% of track width from center

test_cases.name: Entering Edge Zone
test_cases.description: Car is between 25-50% of track width from center

test_cases.name: Very Close to Edge
test_cases.description: Car is over 50% of track width from center (bad positioning)

test_cases.name: Off-Track Crash
test_cases.description: Car has left the track (all wheels off)

test_cases.name: Hard Steering Penalty
test_cases.description: Car is centered but steering is too aggressive (>15 degrees)

test_cases.name: Gentle Steering at Edge
test_cases.description: Car is at edge but steering smoothly

reward_scale.3.5: Excellent - perfectly centered with smooth steering and moderate speed
reward_scale.2.5: Good - close to center with smooth steering and moderate speed
reward_scale.1.5: Okay - approaching edge with smooth speed
reward_scale.1.0: Low - very close to edge but still on track
reward_scale.0.5: Bad - very close to edge and on track

learning_points: Distance from center is the primary factor
Smooth steering (≤15°) is rewarded, harsh steering (>15°) is penalized
The car will learn to drive in the center and steer smoothly
These thresholds (0.1, 0.25, 0.5) are tunable for different strategies
